revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''水神祭'''とは、[[競艇]]において、初勝利や節目の勝利を挙げた[[競艇選手]]が、水面に投げ込まれる行事のこと。 ==詳...'
revision.text:
'''水神祭'''とは、[[競艇]]において、初勝利や節目の勝利を挙げた[[競艇選手]]が、水面に投げ込まれる行事のこと。 ==詳細== *選手になって初めての１着、初優勝（優出）、SGやGⅠのような大レースでの初1着や初優勝、1000勝などの節目の勝利、引退式など、その選手の節目節目のお祝いとして行われる行事であり、基本的にはお祝いを受ける選手が同県同支部の選手や同期の選手の手によって水面に投げ込まれる。 *水神祭が行われる場所は水面に面したピットの端の部分、もしくは掃海艇などで競争水面に出て舟の上から水面に投げる場合もある。ファンサービスとして観客の前で行う事も。 *水神祭のスタイルとしては、お祝いを受ける選手が他の選手達に持ち上げられてうつぶせの状態になり、その姿勢から水面に向かって投げ込まれる「ウルトラマンスタイル」が主流である。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.kyotei.or.jp/ 競艇オフィシャルWeb]
[[Category:競艇|すいじんさい]]
{{stub}}

'''水神祭'''とは、[[競艇]]において、初勝利や節目の勝利を挙げた[[競艇選手]]が、水面に投げ込まれる行事のこと。 ==詳細== *選手になって初めての１着、初優勝、初優出、SGやGⅠのような大レースでの初1着や初優勝、1000勝などの節目の勝利、引退式など、その選手の節目節目のお祝いとして行われる行事であり、基本的にはお祝いを受ける選手が同県同支部の選手や同期の選手の手によって水面に投げ込まれる。 *水神祭が行われる場所は水面に面したピットの端の部分、もしくは掃海艇などで競争水面に出て舟の上から水面に投げる場合もある。ファンサービスとして観客の前で行う事も。 *水神祭のスタイルとしては、お祝いを受ける選手が他の選手達に持ち上げられてうつぶせの状態になり、その姿勢から水面に向かって投げ込まれる「ウルトラマンスタイル」が主流である。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.kyotei.or.jp/ 競艇オフィシャルWeb]
[[Category:競艇|すいじんさい]]
{{stub}}

[[Category:競艇|すいじんさい]]

'''水神祭'''とは、[[競艇]]において、初勝利や節目の勝利を挙げた[[競艇選手]]が、水面に投げ込まれる行事のこと。
==詳細== *選手になって初めての1着、初優勝、初優出、SGやGIのような大レースでの初1着や初優勝、1000勝などの節目の勝利、引退式など、その選手の節目節目のお祝いとして行われる行事であり、基本的にはお祝いを受ける選手が同県同支部の選手や同期の選手の手によって水面に投げ込まれる。 *水神祭が行われる場所は水面に面したピットの端の部分、もしくは掃海艇などで競争水面に出て舟の上から水面に投げる場合もある。ファンサービスとして観客の前で行う事も。 *水神祭のスタイルとしては、お祝いを受ける選手が他の選手達に持ち上げられてうつぶせの状態になり、その姿勢から水面に向かって投げ込まれる「ウルトラマンスタイル」が主流である。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.kyotei.or.jp/ 競艇オフィシャルWeb]
[[Category:競艇|すいじんさい]] {{sports-stub}}

'''水神祭'''とは、[[競艇]]において、初勝利や節目の勝利を挙げた[[競艇選手]]が、水面に投げ込まれる行事のこと。
==詳細== *選手になって初めての1着、初優勝、初優出、SGやGIのような大レースでの初1着や初優勝、1000勝などの節目の勝利、引退式など、その選手の節目節目のお祝いとして行われる行事であり、基本的にはお祝いを受ける選手が同県同支部の選手や同期の選手の手によって水面に投げ込まれる。また、[[やまと競艇学校]]でもリーグ戦や卒業競争で優勝した選手に対して同様の行事が行われている。 *水神祭が行われる場所は水面に面したピットの端の部分、もしくは掃海艇などで競争水面に出て舟の上から水面に投げる場合もある。ファンサービスとして観客の前で行う事も。 *水神祭のスタイルとしては、お祝いを受ける選手が他の選手達に持ち上げられてうつぶせの状態になり、その姿勢から水面に向かって投げ込まれる「ウルトラマンスタイル」が主流である。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.kyotei.or.jp/ 競艇オフィシャルWeb]
[[Category:競艇|すいじんさい]] {{sports-stub}}

==詳細== *選手になって初めての1着、初優勝、初優出、SGやGIのような大レースでの初1着や初優勝、1000勝などの節目の勝利、引退式など、その選手の節目節目のお祝いとして行われる行事であり、基本的にはお祝いを受ける選手が同県同支部の選手や同期の選手の手によって水面に投げ込まれる。また、[[やまと競艇学校]]でもリーグ戦や卒業競争で優勝した選手（[[やまとチャンプ]]）に対して同様の行事が行われている。 *水神祭が行われる場所は水面に面したピットの端の部分、もしくは掃海艇などで競争水面に出て舟の上から水面に投げる場合もある。ファンサービスとして観客の前で行う事も。 *水神祭のスタイルとしては、お祝いを受ける選手が他の選手達に持ち上げられてうつぶせの状態になり、その姿勢から水面に向かって投げ込まれる「ウルトラマンスタイル」が主流である。

# [[水神]]のための[[祭]]のこと。 # [[競艇]]においての行事のこと。本項で詳述。 ---- '''水神祭'''（すいじんさい）とは、[[競艇]]において、初勝利や節目の勝利を挙げた[[競艇選手]]が、水面に投げ込まれる[[行事]]のこと。
== 概要 == 選手になって初めての1着、初優勝、初優出、SGやGIのような大レースでの初1着や初優勝、1000勝などの節目の勝利、引退式など、その選手の節目節目のお祝いとして行われる行事であり、基本的にはお祝いを受ける選手が同県同支部の選手や同期の選手の手によって水面に投げ込まれる。また、[[やまと競艇学校]]でもリーグ戦や卒業競争で優勝した選手（[[やまとチャンプ]]）に対して同様の行事が行われている。
水神祭が行われる場所は水面に面したピットの端の部分、もしくは掃海艇などで競争水面に出て舟の上から水面に投げる場合もある。ファンサービスとして観客の前で行う事も。
水神祭のスタイルとしては、お祝いを受ける選手が他の選手達に持ち上げられてうつぶせの状態になり、その姿勢から水面に向かって投げ込まれる「ウルトラマンスタイル」が主流である。

revision.text:
# [[水神]]のための[[祭]]のこと。 # [[競艇]]においての行事のこと。本項で詳述。 ---- '''水神祭'''（すいじんさい）とは、[[競艇]]において、初勝利や節目の勝利を挙げた[[競艇選手]]が、水面に投げ込まれる[[行事]]のこと。
== 概要 == 選手になって初めての1着、初優勝、初優出、SGやGIのような大レースでの初1着や初優勝、1000勝などの節目の勝利、引退式など、その選手の節目節目のお祝いとして行われる行事であり、基本的にはお祝いを受ける選手が同県同支部の選手や同期の選手の手によって水面に投げ込まれる。また、[[やまと学校|やまと競艇学校]]でもリーグ戦や卒業競争で優勝した選手（[[やまとチャンプ]]）に対して同様の行事が行われている。
水神祭が行われる場所は水面に面したピットの端の部分、もしくは掃海艇などで競争水面に出て舟の上から水面に投げる場合もある。ファンサービスとして観客の前で行う事も。
水神祭のスタイルとしては、お祝いを受ける選手が他の選手達に持ち上げられてうつぶせの状態になり、その姿勢から水面に向かって投げ込まれる「ウルトラマンスタイル」が主流である。

水神祭のスタイルとしては、お祝いを受ける選手が他の選手達に持ち上げられてうつぶせの状態になり、その姿勢から水面に向かって投げ込まれる「ウルトラマンスタイル」が主流であるが巻き込まれる事もしばしばある。

# [[水神]]のための[[祭]]のこと。 # [[競艇]]においての行事のこと。本項で詳述。 ---- '''水神祭'''（すいじんさい）とは、[[競艇]]において、初勝利や節目の勝利を挙げた[[競艇選手]]が、水面に投げ込まれる[[行事]]のこと。
== 概要 == 選手になって初めての1着、初優勝、初優出（初めて優勝戦に進出すること）、SGやGIの様な大きなレースでの初1着や初優勝、1000勝等の節目の勝利、引退式等、その選手の節目節目のお祝いとして行われる行事であり、基本的にはお祝いを受ける選手が同県同支部の選手や同期の選手の手によって水面に投げ込まれる。また、[[やまと学校|やまと競艇学校]]でもリーグ戦や卒業競争で優勝した選手（[[やまとチャンプ]]）に対して同様の行事が行われている。
水神祭が行われる場所は水面に面したピットの端の部分、もしくは掃海艇などで競争水面に出て舟の上から水面に投げる場合もある。ファンサービスとして観客の前で行う事も。
水神祭のスタイルとしては、お祝いを受ける選手が他の選手達に持ち上げられてうつぶせの状態になり、その姿勢から水面に向かって投げ込まれる「ウルトラマンスタイル」が主流であるが巻き込まれる事もしばしばあって場合によっては入る位置が悪ければ怪我を負う事も・・・。

*[[水神]]のための[[祭]]のこと。 *[[競艇]]においての行事のこと。本項で詳述。 ---- '''水神祭'''（すいじんさい）とは、[[競艇]]において、初勝利や節目の勝利を挙げた[[競艇選手]]が、水面に投げ込まれる[[行事]]のことである。
== 概要 == 選手になって初めての1着、初優勝、初優出（初めて優勝戦に進出すること）、SGやGIの様な大きなレースでの初1着や初優勝、1000勝等の節目の勝利、引退式等、その選手の節目節目のお祝いとして行われる行事であり、基本的にはお祝いを受ける選手が同県同支部の選手や同期の選手の手によって水面に投げ込まれる。また、[[やまと学校]]でもリーグ戦や卒業競争で優勝した選手（[[やまとチャンプ]]）に対して同様の行事が行われている。
水神祭が行われる場所は水面に面したピットの端の部分、もしくは掃海艇などで競争水面に出て舟の上から水面に投げる場合もある。ファンサービスとして観客の前で行う事も。
水神祭のスタイルとしては、お祝いを受ける選手が他の選手達に持ち上げられてうつ伏せの状態になり、その姿勢から水面に向かって投げ込まれる「ウルトラマンスタイル」が主流であるが、他のスタイルもある。

*[[水神]]のための[[祭]]のこと。 *[[競艇]]においての行事のこと。本項で詳述。 ---- '''水神祭'''（すいじんさい）とは、[[競艇]]において、初勝利や節目の勝利を挙げた[[競艇選手]]が、水面に投げ込まれる[[行事]]のことである。
== 概要 == 選手になって初めての1着、初優勝、初優出（初めて優勝戦に進出すること）、SGやGIの様な大きなレースでの初1着や初優勝、1000勝等の節目の勝利、引退式等、その選手の節目節目のお祝いとして行われる行事であり、基本的にはお祝いを受ける選手が同県同支部の選手や同期の選手の手によって水面に投げ込まれる。 しかしながら、1982年の[[MB記念]]では、当時すでにSGで6優勝を果たした[[彦坂郁雄]]が水神祭を行っていた映像も残っており、節目のレースだけではなくビッグタイトルを獲得した時にも水神祭が行われていたようである。 また、[[やまと学校]]でもリーグ戦や卒業競争で優勝した選手（[[やまとチャンプ]]）に対して同様の行事が行われている。
水神祭が行われる場所は水面に面したピットの端の部分、もしくは掃海艇などで競争水面に出て舟の上から水面に投げる場合もある。ファンサービスとして観客の前で行う事も。
水神祭のスタイルとしては、お祝いを受ける選手が他の選手達に持ち上げられてうつ伏せの状態になり、その姿勢から水面に向かって投げ込まれる「ウルトラマンスタイル」が主流であるが、他のスタイルもある。

*[[水神]]のための[[祭]]のこと。 *[[競艇]]においての行事のこと。本項で詳述。 ---- '''水神祭'''（すいじんさい）とは、[[競艇]]において、初勝利や節目の勝利を挙げた[[競艇選手]]が、水面に投げ込まれる[[行事]]のことである。
== 概要 == 選手になって初めての1着、初優勝、初優出（初めて優勝戦に進出すること）、SGやGIの様な大きなレースでの初1着や初優勝、1000勝等の節目の勝利、引退式等、その選手の節目節目のお祝いとして行われる行事であり、基本的にはお祝いを受ける選手が同県同支部の選手や同期の選手の手によって水面に投げ込まれる。

*[[水神]]のための[[祭]]のこと。 *[[競艇]]においての行事のこと。本項で詳述。 ---- '''水神祭'''（すいじんさい）とは、[[競艇]]において、初勝利や節目の勝利を挙げた[[競艇選手]]が、水面に投げ込まれる[[行事]]のことである。
== 概要 == 選手になって初めての1着、初優勝、初優出（初めて優勝戦に進出すること）、SGやGIの様な大きなレースでの初1着や初優勝、1000勝等の節目の勝利、引退式等、その選手の節目節目のお祝いとして行われる行事であり、基本的にはお祝いを受ける選手が同県同支部の選手や同期の選手の手によって水面に投げ込まれる。
しかしながら、1982年の[[MB記念]]では、当時すでにSGで6優勝を果たした[[彦坂郁雄]]が水神祭を行っていた映像も残っており、節目のレースだけではなくビッグタイトルを獲得した時にも水神祭が行われていた。
また、[[ボートレーサー養成所]]でもリーグ戦や修了記念競走で優勝した選手（[[養成所チャンプ]]）に対して同様の行事が行われている。
水神祭が行われる場所は水面に面したピットの端の部分、もしくは掃海艇などで競争水面に出て舟の上から水面に投げる場合もある。ファンサービスとして観客の前で行う事もある。
水神祭のスタイルとしては、お祝いを受ける選手が他の選手達に持ち上げられてうつ伏せの状態になり、その姿勢から水面に向かって投げ込まれる「ウルトラマンスタイル」が主流であるが、他のスタイルもある。水神祭は投げ込まれた選手は勿論の事、投げた選手も数人は水面に落ちるシーンが有る。
== 関連項目 == * [[競艇]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kyotei.or.jp/ BOAT RACE official web]
{{Sports-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:すいしんさい}} [[Category:競艇]]

*[[水神]]のための[[祭]]のこと。 *[[競艇]]においての行事のこと。本項で詳述。 ---- '''水神祭'''（すいじんさい）とは、[[競艇]]において、初勝利や節目の勝利を挙げた[[競艇選手]]が、水面に投げ込まれる[[行事]]のことである。
== 概要 == 選手の節目節目のお祝いとして行われる行事であり、基本的にはお祝いを受ける選手が同県同支部の選手や同期の選手の手によって水面に投げ込まれる。
基本的な実施基準としては、次のとおり<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g 水神祭ってなに？│BOATSCOOP] - [[YouTube]] ボートレース公式チャンネル</ref>。 * デビューして初めての1着<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ ボートレース平和島 初勝利 水神祭&表彰 5019 柴田百恵選手] - YouTube [[平和島競艇場]] 公式チャンネル 2018年5月9日</ref>・初優勝・初優出（初めて優勝戦に進出すること） * SGやGIの様な大きなレースでの初1着・初優勝<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG第29回グランドチャンピオン～柳沢一選手 水神祭] - YouTube [[多摩川競艇場]] 公式チャンネル 2019年6月25日</ref> * 1000勝・2000勝等の節目の勝利 * 引退式等
しかしながら、1982年の[[MB記念]]では、当時すでにSGで6優勝を果たした[[彦坂郁雄]]が水神祭を行っていた映像も残っており、節目のレースだけではなくビッグタイトルを獲得した時にも水神祭が行われていた。
また、[[ボートレーサー養成所]]でもリーグ戦や修了記念競走で優勝した選手（[[養成所チャンプ]]）に対して同様の行事が行われている。
水神祭が行われる場所は水面に面したピットの端の部分、もしくは救助艇などで競争水面に出て舟の上から水面に投げる場合もある。ファンサービスとして観客の前で行う事もある。
水神祭のスタイルとしては、お祝いを受ける選手が他の選手達に持ち上げられてうつ伏せの状態になり、その姿勢から水面に向かって投げ込まれる「ウルトラマンスタイル」が主流であるが、他のスタイルもある。水神祭は投げ込まれた選手は勿論の事、投げた選手も数人は水面に落ちるシーンが有る。

*[[水神]]のための[[祭]]のこと。 *[[競艇]]においての行事のこと。本項で詳述。 ---- '''水神祭'''（すいじんさい）とは、[[競艇]]において、初勝利や節目の勝利を挙げた[[競艇選手]]が、水面に投げ込まれる[[行事]]のことである。
== 概要 == 選手の節目節目のお祝いとして行われる行事であり、基本的にはお祝いを受ける選手が同県同支部の選手や同期の選手の手によって水面に投げ込まれる。
基本的な実施基準としては、次のとおり<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g 水神祭ってなに？│BOATSCOOP] - [[YouTube]] ボートレース公式チャンネル</ref>。 * デビューして初めての1着<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ ボートレース平和島 初勝利 水神祭&表彰 5019 柴田百恵選手] - YouTube [[平和島競艇場]] 公式チャンネル 2018年5月9日</ref>・初優勝・初優出（初めて優勝戦に進出すること） * SGやGIの様な大きなレースでの初1着・初優勝<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG第29回グランドチャンピオン～柳沢一選手 水神祭] - YouTube [[多摩川競艇場]] 公式チャンネル 2019年6月25日</ref> * 1000勝・2000勝等の節目の勝利 * 引退式等
最短での水神祭は、BOATRACE殿堂入り第1号の[[今村豊]]のデビュー戦(1981年5月7日)<ref>{{cite web|url= https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=今村豊引退特設サイト|work=ボートレース徳山|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>。

また、[[ボートレーサー養成所]]でもリーグ戦や修了記念競走で優勝した選手（[[養成所チャンプ]]）に対して同様の行事が行われている。
水神祭が行われる場所は水面に面したピットの端の部分、もしくは救助艇などで競争水面に出て舟の上から水面に投げる場合もある。ファンサービスとして観客の前で行う事もある。
水神祭のスタイルとしては、お祝いを受ける選手が他の選手達に持ち上げられてうつ伏せの状態になり、その姿勢から水面に向かって投げ込まれる「ウルトラマンスタイル」が主流であるが、他のスタイルもある。水神祭は投げ込まれた選手は勿論の事、投げた選手も数人は水面に落ちるシーンが有る。

基本的な実施基準としては、次のとおり<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g 水神祭ってなに？│BOATSCOOP] - [[YouTube]] ボートレース公式チャンネル</ref>。 * デビューして初めての1着<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ ボートレース平和島 初勝利 水神祭&表彰 5019 柴田百恵選手] - YouTube [[平和島競艇場]] 公式チャンネル 2018年5月9日</ref>・初優勝・初優出（初めて優勝戦に進出すること） * SGやGIの様な大きなレースでの初1着・初優勝<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG第29回グランドチャンピオン～柳沢一選手 水神祭] - YouTube [[多摩川競艇場]] 公式チャンネル 2019年6月25日</ref> * 1000勝・2000勝等の節目の勝利 * 引退式等
最短での水神祭は、BOATRACE殿堂入り第1号の[[今村豊]]のデビュー戦(1981年5月7日)<ref>{{cite web|url= https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=今村豊引退特設サイト|work=ボートレース徳山|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>。
1982年の[[MB記念]]では、当時すでにSGで6優勝を果たした[[彦坂郁雄]]が水神祭を行っていた映像も残っており、節目のレースだけではなくビッグタイトルを獲得した時にも水神祭が行われていた。
また、[[ボートレーサー養成所]]でもリーグ戦や修了記念競走で優勝した選手（[[養成所チャンプ]]）に対して同様の行事が行われている。
水神祭が行われる場所は水面に面したピットの端の部分、もしくは救助艇などで競争水面に出て舟の上から水面に投げる場合もある。ファンサービスとして観客の前で行う事もある。
水神祭のスタイルとしては、お祝いを受ける選手が他の選手達に持ち上げられてうつ伏せの状態になり、その姿勢から水面に向かって投げ込まれる「ウルトラマンスタイル」が主流であるが、他のスタイルもある。水神祭は投げ込まれた選手は勿論の事、投げた選手も数人は水面に落ちるシーンが有る。
== 関連項目 == * [[競艇]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kyotei.or.jp/ BOAT RACE official web] == 出典 == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
*[[水神]]のための[[祭]]のこと。 *[[競艇]]においての行事のこと。本項で詳述。 ---- '''水神祭'''（すいじんさい）とは、[[競艇]]において、初勝利や節目の勝利を挙げた[[競艇選手]]が、水面に投げ込まれる[[イベント|行事]]のことである。
== 概要 == 選手の節目節目のお祝いとして行われる行事であり、基本的にはお祝いを受ける選手が同県同支部の選手や同期の選手の手によって水面に投げ込まれる。
基本的な実施基準としては、次のとおり<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g 水神祭ってなに？│BOATSCOOP] - [[YouTube]] ボートレース公式チャンネル</ref>。 * デビューして初めての1着<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ ボートレース平和島 初勝利 水神祭&表彰 5019 柴田百恵選手] - YouTube [[平和島競艇場]] 公式チャンネル 2018年5月9日</ref>・初優勝・初優出（初めて優勝戦に進出すること） * SGやGIの様な大きなレースでの初1着・初優勝<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG第29回グランドチャンピオン～柳沢一選手 水神祭] - YouTube [[多摩川競艇場]] 公式チャンネル 2019年6月25日</ref> * 1000勝・2000勝等の節目の勝利 * 引退式等
最短での水神祭は、BOATRACE殿堂入り第1号の[[今村豊]]のデビュー戦(1981年5月7日)<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url= https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=今村豊引退特設サイト|work=ボートレース徳山|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>。
1982年の[[MB記念]]では、当時すでにSGで6優勝を果たした[[彦坂郁雄]]が水神祭を行っていた映像も残っており、節目のレースだけではなくビッグタイトルを獲得した時にも水神祭が行われていた。
また、[[ボートレーサー養成所]]でもリーグ戦や修了記念競走で優勝した選手（[[養成所チャンプ]]）に対して同様の行事が行われている。
水神祭が行われる場所は水面に面したピットの端の部分、もしくは救助艇などで競争水面に出て舟の上から水面に投げる場合もある。ファンサービスとして観客の前で行う事もある。
水神祭のスタイルとしては、お祝いを受ける選手が他の選手達に持ち上げられてうつ伏せの状態になり、その姿勢から水面に向かって投げ込まれる「ウルトラマンスタイル」が主流であるが、他のスタイルもある。水神祭は投げ込まれた選手は勿論の事、投げた選手も数人は水面に落ちるシーンが有る。
== 関連項目 == * [[競艇]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kyotei.or.jp/ BOAT RACE official web] == 出典 == {{Reflist}}

]][[ja:水神祭]]

'''Suijin-sai''' (水神祭) refers to: * A {{ill|festival|ja|祭}} dedicated to the {{ill|water deity|ja|水神}}. * A ceremonial event in {{ill|boat racing|ja|競艇}}, which is detailed in this article.
== Overview ==
In the context of {{ill|boat racing|ja|競艇}}, '''Suijin-sai''' is a celebratory event where a {{ill|boat racer|ja|競艇選手}} who achieves a milestone victory, such as their first win, is thrown into the water by fellow racers.
The basic criteria for holding a Suijin-sai are as follows:<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g What is Suijin-sai? │ BOATSCOOP] - Official {{ill|BOAT RACE|ja|ボートレース}} YouTube Channel</ref>: * First victory after debut<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ BOAT RACE Heiwajima - First Victory Suijin-sai & Award Ceremony for Racer 5019 Momoe Shibata] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|ja|平和島競艇場}}, May 9, 2018</ref>, first championship, or first entry into a final race (初優出). * First win or first championship in major events like {{ill|SG (boat racing)|ja|SG}} or {{ill|GI (boat racing)|ja|GI}} tournaments<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG 29th Grand Champion – Suijin-sai for Kazuki Yanagisawa] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|ja|多摩川競艇場}}, June 25, 2019</ref>. * Milestone victories such as 1,000th or 2,000th win. * Retirement ceremonies.
The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.
Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Boat racing|ja|競艇}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kyotei.or.jp/ BOAT RACE Official Website]
== References == {{Reflist}}
{{Sports-stub}}
[[Category:Boat racing]] [[ja:水神祭]]

The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.

'''Suijin-sai''' (水神祭) refers to: * A {{ill|festival|ja|祭}} dedicated to the {{ill|water deity|ja|水神}}. * A ceremonial event in {{ill|boat racing|ja|競艇}}, which is detailed in this article.
== Overview ==
In the context of {{ill|boat racing|ja|競艇}}, '''Suijin-sai''' is a celebratory event where a {{ill|boat racer|ja|競艇選手}} who achieves a milestone victory, such as their first win, is thrown into the water by fellow racers.
The basic criteria for holding a Suijin-sai are as follows:<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g What is Suijin-sai? │ BOATSCOOP] - Official {{ill|BOAT RACE|ja|ボートレース}} YouTube Channel</ref>: * First victory after debut<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ BOAT RACE Heiwajima - First Victory Suijin-sai & Award Ceremony for Racer 5019 Momoe Shibata] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|ja|平和島競艇場}}, May 9, 2018</ref>, first championship, or first entry into a final race (初優出). * First win or first championship in major events like {{ill|SG (boat racing)|ja|SG}} or {{ill|GI (boat racing)|ja|GI}} tournaments<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG 29th Grand Champion – Suijin-sai for Kazuki Yanagisawa] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|ja|多摩川競艇場}}, June 25, 2019</ref>. * Milestone victories such as 1,000th or 2,000th win. * Retirement ceremonies.
The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.
Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.

revision.text:
'''Suijin-sai''' (水神祭) refers to: * A {{ill|festival|ja|祭}} dedicated to the {{ill|water deity|ja|水神}}. * A ceremonial event in {{ill|boat racing|ja|競艇}}, which is detailed in this article.
== Overview ==
In the context of {{ill|boat racing|ja|競艇}}, '''Suijin-sai''' is a celebratory event where a {{ill|boat racer|ja|競艇選手}} who achieves a milestone victory, such as their first win, is thrown into the water by fellow racers.
The basic criteria for holding a Suijin-sai are as follows:<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g What is Suijin-sai? │ BOATSCOOP] - Official {{ill|BOAT RACE|ja|ボートレース}} YouTube Channel</ref>: * First victory after debut<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ BOAT RACE Heiwajima - First Victory Suijin-sai & Award Ceremony for Racer 5019 Momoe Shibata] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|ja|平和島競艇場}}, May 9, 2018</ref>, first championship, or first entry into a final race (初優出). * First win or first championship in major events like {{ill|SG (boat racing)|ja|SG}} or {{ill|GI (boat racing)|ja|GI}} tournaments<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG 29th Grand Champion – Suijin-sai for Kazuki Yanagisawa] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|ja|多摩川競艇場}}, June 25, 2019</ref>. * Milestone victories such as 1,000th or 2,000th win. * Retirement ceremonies.
The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.
Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Boat racing|ja|競艇}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kyotei.or.jp/ BOAT RACE Official Website]
== References == {{Reflist}}
{{Sports-stub}}

Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.

'''Suijin-sai''' (水神祭) refers to: * A {{ill|festival|ja|祭}} dedicated to the {{ill|water deity|ja|水神}}. * A ceremonial event in {{ill|boat racing|ja|競艇}}, which is detailed in this article.
== Overview ==
In the context of {{ill|boat racing|ja|競艇}}, '''Suijin-sai''' is a celebratory event where a {{ill|boat racer|ja|競艇選手}} who achieves a milestone victory, such as their first win, is thrown into the water by fellow racers.
The basic criteria for holding a Suijin-sai are as follows:<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g What is Suijin-sai? │ BOATSCOOP] - Official {{ill|BOAT RACE|ja|ボートレース}} YouTube Channel</ref>: * First victory after debut<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ BOAT RACE Heiwajima - First Victory Suijin-sai & Award Ceremony for Racer 5019 Momoe Shibata] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|ja|平和島競艇場}}, May 9, 2018</ref>, first championship, or first entry into a final race (初優出). * First win or first championship in major events like {{ill|SG (boat racing)|ja|SG}} or {{ill|GI (boat racing)|ja|GI}} tournaments<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG 29th Grand Champion – Suijin-sai for Kazuki Yanagisawa] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|ja|多摩川競艇場}}, June 25, 2019</ref>. * Milestone victories such as 1,000th or 2,000th win. * Retirement ceremonies.
The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.
Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Deprecated qq open]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Suijin-sai''' (水神祭) refers to: * A {{ill|festival|ja|祭}} dedicated to the {{ill|water deity|ja|水神}}. * A ceremonial event in {{ill|boat racing|ja|競艇}}, which is detailed in this article.
== Overview ==
In the context of {{ill|boat racing|ja|競艇}}, '''Suijin-sai''' is a celebratory event where a {{ill|boat racer|ja|競艇選手}} who achieves a milestone victory, such as their first win, is thrown into the water by fellow racers.
The basic criteria for holding a Suijin-sai are as follows:<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g What is Suijin-sai? │ BOATSCOOP] - Official {{ill|BOAT RACE|ja|ボートレース}} YouTube Channel</ref>: * First victory after debut<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ BOAT RACE Heiwajima - First Victory Suijin-sai & Award Ceremony for Racer 5019 Momoe Shibata] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|ja|平和島競艇場}}, May 9, 2018</ref>, first championship, or first entry into a final race (初優出). * First win or first championship in major events like {{ill|SG (boat racing)|ja|SG}} or {{ill|GI (boat racing)|ja|GI}} tournaments<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG 29th Grand Champion – Suijin-sai for Kazuki Yanagisawa] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|ja|多摩川競艇場}}, June 25, 2019</ref>. * Milestone victories such as 1,000th or 2,000th win. * Retirement ceremonies.
The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.
Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Boat racing|ja|競艇}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kyotei.or.jp/ BOAT RACE Official Website]
== References == {{Reflist}}
{{Sports-stub}}

== Overview ==
In the context of {{ill|boat racing|ja|競艇}}, '''Suijin-sai''' is a celebratory event where a {{ill|boat racer|ja|競艇選手}} who achieves a milestone victory, such as their first win, is thrown into the water by fellow racers.
The basic criteria for holding a Suijin-sai are as follows:<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g What is Suijin-sai? │ BOATSCOOP] - Official {{ill|BOAT RACE|ja|ボートレース}} YouTube Channel</ref>: * First victory after debut<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ BOAT RACE Heiwajima - First Victory Suijin-sai & Award Ceremony for Racer 5019 Momoe Shibata] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|ja|平和島競艇場}}, May 9, 2018</ref>, first championship, or first entry into a final race (初優出). * First win or first championship in major events like {{ill|SG (boat racing)|ja|SG}} or {{ill|GI (boat racing)|ja|GI}} tournaments<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG 29th Grand Champion – Suijin-sai for Kazuki Yanagisawa] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|ja|多摩川競艇場}}, June 25, 2019</ref>. * Milestone victories such as 1,000th or 2,000th win. * Retirement ceremonies.
The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.
Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Boat racing|ja|競艇}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kyotei.or.jp/ BOAT RACE Official Website]
== References == {{Reflist}}
{{Sports-stub}}

== Overview ==
In the context of {{ill|boat racing|ja|競艇}}, '''Suijin-sai''' is a celebratory event where a {{ill|boat racer|ja|競艇選手}} who achieves a milestone victory, such as their first win, is thrown into the water by fellow racers.
The basic criteria for holding a Suijin-sai are as follows:<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g What is Suijin-sai? │ BOATSCOOP] - Official {{ill|BOAT RACE|ja|ボートレース}} YouTube Channel</ref>: * First victory after debut<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ BOAT RACE Heiwajima - First Victory Suijin-sai & Award Ceremony for Racer 5019 Momoe Shibata] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|ja|平和島競艇場}}, May 9, 2018</ref>, first championship, or first entry into a final race (初優出). * First win or first championship in major events like {{ill|SG (boat racing)|ja|SG}} or {{ill|GI (boat racing)|ja|GI}} tournaments<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG 29th Grand Champion – Suijin-sai for Kazuki Yanagisawa] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|ja|多摩川競艇場}}, June 25, 2019</ref>. * Milestone victories such as 1,000th or 2,000th win. * Retirement ceremonies.
The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.
Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Boat racing|ja|競艇}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kyotei.or.jp/ BOAT RACE Official Website]
== References == {{Reflist}}
{{Sports-stub}}

== Overview ==
In the context of {{ill|boat racing|ja|競艇|12=draft}}, '''Suijin-sai''' is a celebratory event where a {{ill|boat racer|ja|競艇選手|12=draft}} who achieves a milestone victory, such as their first win, is thrown into the water by fellow racers.
The basic criteria for holding a Suijin-sai are as follows:<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g What is Suijin-sai? │ BOATSCOOP] - Official {{ill|BOAT RACE|ja|ボートレース|12=draft}} YouTube Channel</ref>: * First victory after debut<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ BOAT RACE Heiwajima - First Victory Suijin-sai & Award Ceremony for Racer 5019 Momoe Shibata] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|ja|平和島競艇場|12=draft}}, May 9, 2018</ref>, first championship, or first entry into a final race (初優出). * First win or first championship in major events like {{ill|SG (boat racing)|ja|SG|12=draft}} or {{ill|GI (boat racing)|ja|GI|12=draft}} tournaments<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG 29th Grand Champion – Suijin-sai for Kazuki Yanagisawa] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|ja|多摩川競艇場|12=draft}}, June 25, 2019</ref>. * Milestone victories such as 1,000th or 2,000th win. * Retirement ceremonies.
The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊|12=draft}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念|12=draft}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄|12=draft}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.
Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所|12=draft}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ|12=draft}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Boat racing|ja|競艇|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kyotei.or.jp/ BOAT RACE Official Website]
== References == {{Reflist}}
{{Sports-stub}}

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
25 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:水神祭 up to rev 98160777
revision.text:
*[[水神]]のための[[祭]]のこと。 *[[競艇]]においての行事のこと。本項で詳述。 ---- '''水神祭'''（すいじんさい）とは、[[競艇]]において、初勝利や節目の勝利を挙げた[[競艇選手]]が、水面に投げ込まれる[[イベント|行事]]のことである。
== 概要 == 選手の節目節目のお祝いとして行われる行事であり、基本的にはお祝いを受ける選手が同県同支部の選手や同期の選手の手によって水面に投げ込まれる。
基本的な実施基準としては、次のとおり<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g 水神祭ってなに？│BOATSCOOP] - [[YouTube]] ボートレース公式チャンネル</ref>。 * デビューして初めての1着<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ ボートレース平和島 初勝利 水神祭&表彰 5019 柴田百恵選手] - YouTube [[平和島競艇場]] 公式チャンネル 2018年5月9日</ref>・初優勝・初優出（初めて優勝戦に進出すること） * SGやGIの様な大きなレースでの初1着・初優勝<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG第29回グランドチャンピオン～柳沢一選手 水神祭] - YouTube [[多摩川競艇場]] 公式チャンネル 2019年6月25日</ref> * 1000勝・2000勝等の節目の勝利 * 引退式等
最短での水神祭は、BOATRACE殿堂入り第1号の[[今村豊]]のデビュー戦(1981年5月7日)<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url= https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=今村豊引退特設サイト|work=ボートレース徳山|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>。
1982年の[[MB記念]]では、当時すでにSGで6優勝を果たした[[彦坂郁雄]]が水神祭を行っていた映像も残っており、節目のレースだけではなくビッグタイトルを獲得した時にも水神祭が行われていた。
また、[[ボートレーサー養成所]]でもリーグ戦や修了記念競走で優勝した選手（[[養成所チャンプ]]）に対して同様の行事が行われている。
水神祭が行われる場所は水面に面したピットの端の部分、もしくは救助艇などで競争水面に出て舟の上から水面に投げる場合もある。ファンサービスとして観客の前で行う事もある。
水神祭のスタイルとしては、お祝いを受ける選手が他の選手達に持ち上げられてうつ伏せの状態になり、その姿勢から水面に向かって投げ込まれる「ウルトラマンスタイル」が主流であるが、他のスタイルもある。水神祭は投げ込まれた選手は勿論の事、投げた選手も数人は水面に落ちるシーンが有る。
== 関連項目 == * [[競艇]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kyotei.or.jp/ BOAT RACE official web] == 出典 == {{Reflist}}
{{Sports-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:すいしんさい}} [[Category:競艇]]

== Overview ==
In the context of {{ill|boat racing|ja|競艇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, '''Suijin-sai''' is a celebratory event where a {{ill|boat racer|ja|競艇選手|qq=draft|qq=draft}} who achieves a milestone victory, such as their first win, is thrown into the water by fellow racers.
The basic criteria for holding a Suijin-sai are as follows:<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g What is Suijin-sai? │ BOATSCOOP] - Official {{ill|BOAT RACE|ja|ボートレース|qq=draft|qq=draft}} YouTube Channel</ref>: * First victory after debut<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ BOAT RACE Heiwajima - First Victory Suijin-sai & Award Ceremony for Racer 5019 Momoe Shibata] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|ja|平和島競艇場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, May 9, 2018</ref>, first championship, or first entry into a final race (初優出). * First win or first championship in major events like {{ill|SG (boat racing)|ja|SG|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|GI (boat racing)|ja|GI|qq=draft|qq=draft}} tournaments<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG 29th Grand Champion – Suijin-sai for Kazuki Yanagisawa] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|ja|多摩川競艇場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, June 25, 2019</ref>. * Milestone victories such as 1,000th or 2,000th win. * Retirement ceremonies.
The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念|qq=draft|qq=draft}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.
Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Boat racing|ja|競艇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kyotei.or.jp/ BOAT RACE Official Website]

== Overview ==
In the context of [[:en:Kyōtei|boat racing]], '''Suijin-sai''' is a celebratory event where a {{ill|boat racer|ja|競艇選手|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/boat racer|qq=}} who achieves a milestone victory, such as their first win, is thrown into the water by fellow racers.
The basic criteria for holding a Suijin-sai are as follows:<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g What is Suijin-sai? │ BOATSCOOP] - Official [[:en:Boat race (disambiguation)|BOAT RACE]] YouTube Channel</ref>: * First victory after debut<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ BOAT RACE Heiwajima - First Victory Suijin-sai & Award Ceremony for Racer 5019 Momoe Shibata] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|ja|平和島競艇場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|qq=}}, May 9, 2018</ref>, first championship, or first entry into a final race (初優出). * First win or first championship in major events like [[:en:SG|SG (boat racing)]] or [[:en:GI|GI (boat racing)]] tournaments<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG 29th Grand Champion – Suijin-sai for Kazuki Yanagisawa] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|ja|多摩川競艇場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|qq=}}, June 25, 2019</ref>. * Milestone victories such as 1,000th or 2,000th win. * Retirement ceremonies.
The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutaka Imamura|qq=}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MB Memorial|qq=}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuo Hikosaka|qq=}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.
Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat Racer Training School|qq=}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Training School Champ|qq=}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Kyōtei|Boat racing]]
== External Links == * [http://www.kyotei.or.jp/ BOAT RACE Official Website]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11549052]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Suijin-sai''' (水神祭) refers to: * A [[:en:Festival|Matsuri]] dedicated to the [[:en:Suijin|water god]]. * A ceremonial event in [[:en:Kyōtei|boat racing]], which is detailed in this article.
== Overview ==
In the context of [[:en:Kyōtei|boat racing]], '''Suijin-sai''' is a celebratory event where a {{ill|boat racer|ja|競艇選手|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/boat racer|qq=|1=boat racer|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} who achieves a milestone victory, such as their first win, is thrown into the water by fellow racers.
The basic criteria for holding a Suijin-sai are as follows:<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g What is Suijin-sai? │ BOATSCOOP] - Official [[:en:Boat race (disambiguation)|BOAT RACE]] YouTube Channel</ref>: * First victory after debut<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ BOAT RACE Heiwajima - First Victory Suijin-sai & Award Ceremony for Racer 5019 Momoe Shibata] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|ja|平和島競艇場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|qq=|1=Boat Race Heiwajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata}}, May 9, 2018</ref>, first championship, or first entry into a final race (初優出). * First win or first championship in major events like [[:en:SG|SG (boat racing)]] or [[:en:GI|GI (boat racing)]] tournaments<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG 29th Grand Champion – Suijin-sai for Kazuki Yanagisawa] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|ja|多摩川競艇場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|qq=|1=Tamagawa Boat Race|comment=changed title based on wikidata}}, June 25, 2019</ref>. * Milestone victories such as 1,000th or 2,000th win. * Retirement ceremonies.
The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutaka Imamura|qq=|1=Yutaka Imamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MB Memorial|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モーターボート記念競走}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuo Hikosaka|qq=|1=Ikuo Hikosaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.
Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat Racer Training School|qq=|1=Yamato School|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Training School Champ|qq=}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Kyōtei|Boat racing]]
== External Links == * [http://www.kyotei.or.jp/ BOAT RACE Official Website]

The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutaka Imamura|qq=|1=Yutaka Imamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MB Memorial|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モーターボート記念競走}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuo Hikosaka|qq=|1=Ikuo Hikosaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.
Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat Racer Training School|qq=|1=Yamato School|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Training School Champ|qq=}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.

== Overview ==
In the context of [[:en:Kyōtei|boat racing]], '''Suijin-sai''' is a celebratory event where a {{ill|boat racer|ja|競艇選手|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/boat racer|qq=|1=boat racer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598549}} who achieves a milestone victory, such as their first win, is thrown into the water by fellow racers.
The basic criteria for holding a Suijin-sai are as follows:<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g What is Suijin-sai? │ BOATSCOOP] - Official [[:en:Boat race (disambiguation)|BOAT RACE]] YouTube Channel</ref>: * First victory after debut<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ BOAT RACE Heiwajima - First Victory Suijin-sai & Award Ceremony for Racer 5019 Momoe Shibata] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|ja|平和島競艇場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|qq=|1=Boat Race Heiwajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482238}}, May 9, 2018</ref>, first championship, or first entry into a final race (初優出). * First win or first championship in major events like [[:en:SG|SG (boat racing)]] or [[:en:GI|GI (boat racing)]] tournaments<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG 29th Grand Champion – Suijin-sai for Kazuki Yanagisawa] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|ja|多摩川競艇場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|qq=|1=Tamagawa Boat Race|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430752}}, June 25, 2019</ref>. * Milestone victories such as 1,000th or 2,000th win. * Retirement ceremonies.
The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutaka Imamura|qq=|1=Yutaka Imamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377115}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MB Memorial|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モーターボート記念競走|1=MB Memorial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11344829}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuo Hikosaka|qq=|1=Ikuo Hikosaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487908}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.
Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat Racer Training School|qq=|1=Yamato School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280518}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Training School Champ|qq=|1=Training School Champ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280515}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.

== Overview ==
In the context of [[:en:Kyōtei|boat racing]], '''Suijin-sai''' is a celebratory event where a {{ill|boat racer|ja|競艇選手|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/boat racer|qq=|1=boat racer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598549}} who achieves a milestone victory, such as their first win, is thrown into the water by fellow racers.
The basic criteria for holding a Suijin-sai are as follows:<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g What is Suijin-sai? │ BOATSCOOP] - Official [[:en:Boat race (disambiguation)|BOAT RACE]] YouTube Channel</ref>: * First victory after debut<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ BOAT RACE Heiwajima - First Victory Suijin-sai & Award Ceremony for Racer 5019 Momoe Shibata] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|ja|平和島競艇場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|qq=|1=Boat Race Heiwajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482238}}, May 9, 2018</ref>, first championship, or first entry into a final race (初優出). * First win or first championship in major events like [[:en:SG|SG (boat racing)]] or [[:en:GI|GI (boat racing)]] tournaments<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG 29th Grand Champion – Suijin-sai for Kazuki Yanagisawa] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|ja|多摩川競艇場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|qq=|1=Tamagawa Boat Race|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430752}}, June 25, 2019</ref>. * Milestone victories such as 1,000th or 2,000th win. * Retirement ceremonies.
The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutaka Imamura|qq=|1=Yutaka Imamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377115}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MB Memorial|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モーターボート記念競走|1=MB Memorial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11344829}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuo Hikosaka|qq=|1=Ikuo Hikosaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487908}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.
Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat Racer Training School|qq=|1=Yamato School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280518}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Training School Champ|qq=|1=Training School Champ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280515}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.

In the context of [[:en:Kyōtei|boat racing]], '''Suijin-sai''' is a celebratory event where a {{ill|boat racer|ja|競艇選手|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/boat racer|1=boat racer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598549}} who achieves a milestone victory, such as their first win, is thrown into the water by fellow racers.
The basic criteria for holding a Suijin-sai are as follows:<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g What is Suijin-sai? │ BOATSCOOP] - Official [[:en:Boat race (disambiguation)|BOAT RACE]] YouTube Channel</ref>: * First victory after debut<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ BOAT RACE Heiwajima - First Victory Suijin-sai & Award Ceremony for Racer 5019 Momoe Shibata] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|ja|平和島競艇場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|1=Boat Race Heiwajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482238}}, May 9, 2018</ref>, first championship, or first entry into a final race (初優出). * First win or first championship in major events like [[:en:SG|SG (boat racing)]] or [[:en:GI|GI (boat racing)]] tournaments<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG 29th Grand Champion – Suijin-sai for Kazuki Yanagisawa] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|ja|多摩川競艇場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|1=Tamagawa Boat Race|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430752}}, June 25, 2019</ref>. * Milestone victories such as 1,000th or 2,000th win. * Retirement ceremonies.
The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutaka Imamura|1=Yutaka Imamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377115}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MB Memorial|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モーターボート記念競走|1=MB Memorial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11344829}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuo Hikosaka|1=Ikuo Hikosaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487908}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.
Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat Racer Training School|1=Yamato School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280518}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Training School Champ|1=Training School Champ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280515}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.

The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.

The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutaka Imamura|1=Yutaka Imamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377115}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MB Memorial|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モーターボート記念競走|1=MB Memorial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11344829}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuo Hikosaka|1=Ikuo Hikosaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487908}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.
Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat Racer Training School|1=Yamato School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280518}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Training School Champ|1=Training School Champ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280515}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:水神祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11549052]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Suijin-sai''' (水神祭) refers to: * A [[:en:Festival|Matsuri]] dedicated to the [[:en:Suijin|water god]]. * A ceremonial event in [[:en:Kyōtei|boat racing]], which is detailed in this article.
== Overview ==
In the context of [[:en:Kyōtei|boat racing]], '''Suijin-sai''' is a celebratory event where a {{ill|boat racer|ja|競艇選手|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/boat racer|1=boat racer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598549}} who achieves a milestone victory, such as their first win, is thrown into the water by fellow racers.
The basic criteria for holding a Suijin-sai are as follows:<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g What is Suijin-sai? │ BOATSCOOP] - Official [[:en:Boat race (disambiguation)|BOAT RACE]] YouTube Channel</ref>: * First victory after debut<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ BOAT RACE Heiwajima - First Victory Suijin-sai & Award Ceremony for Racer 5019 Momoe Shibata] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|ja|平和島競艇場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|1=Boat Race Heiwajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482238}}, May 9, 2018</ref>, first championship, or first entry into a final race (初優出). * First win or first championship in major events like [[:en:SG|SG (boat racing)]] or [[:en:GI|GI (boat racing)]] tournaments<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG 29th Grand Champion – Suijin-sai for Kazuki Yanagisawa] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|ja|多摩川競艇場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|1=Tamagawa Boat Race|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430752}}, June 25, 2019</ref>. * Milestone victories such as 1,000th or 2,000th win. * Retirement ceremonies.
The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutaka Imamura|1=Yutaka Imamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377115}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MB Memorial|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モーターボート記念競走|1=MB Memorial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11344829}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuo Hikosaka|1=Ikuo Hikosaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487908}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.
Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat Racer Training School|1=Yamato School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280518}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Training School Champ|1=Training School Champ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280515}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.

== Overview ==
In the context of [[:en:Kyōtei|boat racing]], '''Suijin-sai''' is a celebratory event where a {{ill|boat racer|ja|競艇選手|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/boat racer|1=boat racer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598549}} who achieves a milestone victory, such as their first win, is thrown into the water by fellow racers.
The basic criteria for holding a Suijin-sai are as follows:<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g What is Suijin-sai? │ BOATSCOOP] - Official [[:en:Boat race (disambiguation)|BOAT RACE]] YouTube Channel</ref>: * First victory after debut<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ BOAT RACE Heiwajima - First Victory Suijin-sai & Award Ceremony for Racer 5019 Momoe Shibata] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|ja|平和島競艇場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|1=Boat Race Heiwajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482238}}, May 9, 2018</ref>, first championship, or first entry into a final race (初優出). * First win or first championship in major events like [[:en:SG|SG (boat racing)]] or [[:en:GI|GI (boat racing)]] tournaments<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG 29th Grand Champion – Suijin-sai for Kazuki Yanagisawa] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|ja|多摩川競艇場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|1=Tamagawa Boat Race|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430752}}, June 25, 2019</ref>. * Milestone victories such as 1,000th or 2,000th win. * Retirement ceremonies.
The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutaka Imamura|1=Yutaka Imamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377115}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MB Memorial|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モーターボート記念競走|1=MB Memorial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11344829}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuo Hikosaka|1=Ikuo Hikosaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487908}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.
Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat Racer Training School|1=Yamato School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280518}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Training School Champ|1=Training School Champ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280515}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Kyōtei|Boat racing]]
== External Links == * [http://www.kyotei.or.jp/ BOAT RACE Official Website]

== Overview ==
In the context of [[:en:Kyōtei|boat racing]], '''Suijin-sai''' is a celebratory event where a {{ill|boat racer|ja|競艇選手|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/boat racer|1=boat racer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598549}} who achieves a milestone victory, such as their first win, is thrown into the water by fellow racers.
The basic criteria for holding a Suijin-sai are as follows:<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g What is Suijin-sai? │ BOATSCOOP] - Official [[:en:Boat race (disambiguation)|BOAT RACE]] YouTube Channel</ref>: * First victory after debut<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ BOAT RACE Heiwajima - First Victory Suijin-sai & Award Ceremony for Racer 5019 Momoe Shibata] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|ja|平和島競艇場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|1=Boat Race Heiwajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482238}}, May 9, 2018</ref>, first championship, or first entry into a final race (初優出). * First win or first championship in major events like [[:en:SG|SG (boat racing)]] or [[:en:GI|GI (boat racing)]] tournaments<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG 29th Grand Champion – Suijin-sai for Kazuki Yanagisawa] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|ja|多摩川競艇場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|1=Tamagawa Boat Race|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430752}}, June 25, 2019</ref>. * Milestone victories such as 1,000th or 2,000th win. * Retirement ceremonies.
The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutaka Imamura|1=Yutaka Imamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377115}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MB Memorial|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モーターボート記念競走|1=MB Memorial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11344829}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuo Hikosaka|1=Ikuo Hikosaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487908}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.
Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat Racer Training School|1=Yamato School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280518}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Training School Champ|1=Training School Champ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280515}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.

The basic criteria for holding a Suijin-sai are as follows:<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g What is Suijin-sai? │ BOATSCOOP] - Official [[:en:Boat race (disambiguation)|BOAT RACE]] YouTube Channel</ref>: * First victory after debut<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ BOAT RACE Heiwajima - First Victory Suijin-sai & Award Ceremony for Racer 5019 Momoe Shibata] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|ja|平和島競艇場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|1=Boat Race Heiwajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482238}}, May 9, 2018</ref>, first championship, or first entry into a final race (初優出). * First win or first championship in major events like [[:en:SG|SG (boat racing)]] or [[:en:GI|GI (boat racing)]] tournaments<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG 29th Grand Champion – Suijin-sai for Kazuki Yanagisawa] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|ja|多摩川競艇場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|1=Tamagawa Boat Race|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430752}}, June 25, 2019</ref>. * Milestone victories such as 1,000th or 2,000th win. * Retirement ceremonies.
The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutaka Imamura|1=Yutaka Imamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377115}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MB Memorial|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モーターボート記念競走|1=MB Memorial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11344829}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuo Hikosaka|1=Ikuo Hikosaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487908}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.

The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.

== Overview ==
In the context of [[:en:Kyōtei|boat racing]], '''Suijin-sai''' is a celebratory event where a {{ill|boat racer|ja|競艇選手|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/boat racer|1=boat racer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598549}} who achieves a milestone victory, such as their first win, is thrown into the water by fellow racers.
The basic criteria for holding a Suijin-sai are as follows:<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g What is Suijin-sai? │ BOATSCOOP] - Official [[:en:Boat race (disambiguation)|BOAT RACE]] YouTube Channel</ref>: * First victory after debut<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ BOAT RACE Heiwajima - First Victory Suijin-sai & Award Ceremony for Racer 5019 Momoe Shibata] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|ja|平和島競艇場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|1=Boat Race Heiwajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482238}}, May 9, 2018</ref>, first championship, or first entry into a final race (初優出). * First win or first championship in major events like [[:en:SG|SG (boat racing)]] or [[:en:GI|GI (boat racing)]] tournaments<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG 29th Grand Champion – Suijin-sai for Kazuki Yanagisawa] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|ja|多摩川競艇場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|1=Tamagawa Boat Race|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430752}}, June 25, 2019</ref>. * Milestone victories such as 1,000th or 2,000th win. * Retirement ceremonies.
The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutaka Imamura|1=Yutaka Imamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377115}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MB Memorial|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モーターボート記念競走|1=MB Memorial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11344829}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuo Hikosaka|1=Ikuo Hikosaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487908}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.
Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat Racer Training School|1=Yamato School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280518}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Training School Champ|1=Training School Champ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280515}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.

The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutaka Imamura|1=Yutaka Imamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377115}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>

The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.
