== Overview ==
In the context of {{ill|Kyōtei|en|Kyōtei|WD=Q1056235|lt=boat racing}}, '''Suijin-sai''' is a celebratory event where a {{ill|boat racer|ja|競艇選手|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/boat racer|1=boat racer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598549}} who achieves a milestone victory, such as their first win, is thrown into the water by fellow racers.
The basic criteria for holding a Suijin-sai are as follows:<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g What is Suijin-sai? │ BOATSCOOP] - Official {{ill|Boat race (disambiguation)|en|Boat race (disambiguation)|WD=Q4931511|lt=BOAT RACE}} YouTube Channel</ref>: * First victory after debut<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ BOAT RACE Heiwajima - First Victory Suijin-sai & Award Ceremony for Racer 5019 Momoe Shibata] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|ja|平和島競艇場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|1=Boat Race Heiwajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482238}}, May 9, 2018</ref>, first championship, or first entry into a final race (初優出). * First win or first championship in major events like {{ill|SG|en|SG|WD=Q219249|lt=SG (boat racing)}} or {{ill|GI|en|GI|WD=Q224267|lt=GI (boat racing)}} tournaments<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG 29th Grand Champion – Suijin-sai for Kazuki Yanagisawa] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|ja|多摩川競艇場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|1=Tamagawa Boat Race|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430752}}, June 25, 2019</ref>. * Milestone victories such as 1,000th or 2,000th win. * Retirement ceremonies.
The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutaka Imamura|1=Yutaka Imamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377115}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MB Memorial|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モーターボート記念競走|1=MB Memorial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11344829}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuo Hikosaka|1=Ikuo Hikosaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487908}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.
Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat Racer Training School|1=Yamato School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280518}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Training School Champ|1=Training School Champ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280515}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.

The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutaka Imamura|1=Yutaka Imamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377115}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>

'''Suijin-sai''' (水神祭) refers to: * A {{ill|Festival|en|Festival|WD=Q132241|lt=Matsuri|1=Festival}} dedicated to the {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god|1=Suijin}}. * A ceremonial event in {{ill|Kyōtei|en|Kyōtei|WD=Q1056235|lt=boat racing|1=Kyōtei}}, which is detailed in this article.
== Overview ==
In the context of {{ill|Kyōtei|en|Kyōtei|WD=Q1056235|lt=boat racing|1=Kyōtei}}, '''Suijin-sai''' is a celebratory event where a {{ill|boat racer|ja|競艇選手|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/boat racer|1=boat racer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598549}} who achieves a milestone victory, such as their first win, is thrown into the water by fellow racers.
The basic criteria for holding a Suijin-sai are as follows:<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oj6KQKk522g What is Suijin-sai? │ BOATSCOOP] - Official {{ill|Boat race (disambiguation)|en|Boat race (disambiguation)|WD=Q4931511|lt=BOAT RACE|1=Boat race (disambiguation)}} YouTube Channel</ref>: * First victory after debut<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAKGx7ZxPMQ BOAT RACE Heiwajima - First Victory Suijin-sai & Award Ceremony for Racer 5019 Momoe Shibata] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|ja|平和島競艇場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiwajima Boat Racecourse|1=Boat Race Heiwajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482238}}, May 9, 2018</ref>, first championship, or first entry into a final race (初優出). * First win or first championship in major events like {{ill|SG|en|SG|WD=Q219249|lt=SG (boat racing)|1=SG}} or {{ill|GI|en|GI|WD=Q224267|lt=GI (boat racing)|1=GI}} tournaments<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sRpCXcxe4FQ SG 29th Grand Champion – Suijin-sai for Kazuki Yanagisawa] - YouTube, Official Channel of {{ill|Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|ja|多摩川競艇場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Boat Racecourse|1=Tamagawa Boat Race|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430752}}, June 25, 2019</ref>. * Milestone victories such as 1,000th or 2,000th win. * Retirement ceremonies.
The earliest recorded Suijin-sai was for {{ill|Yutaka Imamura|ja|今村豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutaka Imamura|1=Yutaka Imamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377115}}, inducted into the BOATRACE Hall of Fame, held after his debut race on May 7, 1981.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yutakaimamura2992.com/|title=Yutaka Imamura Retirement Special Site|work=BOAT RACE Tokuyama|accessdate=2022-05-31}}</ref>
A video from the 1982 {{ill|MB Memorial|ja|MB記念|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MB Memorial|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モーターボート記念競走|1=MB Memorial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11344829}} shows {{ill|Ikuo Hikosaka|ja|彦坂郁雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuo Hikosaka|1=Ikuo Hikosaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487908}}, who had already won six SG titles, participating in a Suijin-sai, indicating that the event was not limited to milestone races but also celebrated major title victories.
Similar ceremonies are held at the {{ill|Boat Racer Training School|ja|ボートレーサー養成所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat Racer Training School|1=Yamato School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280518}} for students who win league competitions or graduation commemorative races, known as the {{ill|Training School Champ|ja|養成所チャンプ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Training School Champ|1=Training School Champ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280515}}.
The event typically takes place at the edge of the pit area facing the water, or from rescue boats where racers are thrown into the water. Sometimes, for fan service, it is performed in front of the audience.
The most common style of Suijin-sai is the "Ultraman Style," where the celebrated racer is lifted in a face-down position and thrown into the water. Other variations exist, and it's common for not only the honoree but also some of the throwers to end up in the water.
